name: GPT-4
brainrotOneliner: architecture details undisclosed. probably MoE. passed the bar exam. currently employed at every startup.
keyInnovations: RLHF + PPO alignment pipeline made the model dramatically more useful and safe
Multimodal input capability — processes images alongside text
System prompt adherence and instruction-following at a qualitatively different level
Context window scaled to 128k tokens with good long-range coherence
brainrotSummary: GPT-4 is the model where OpenAI went full classified. they won't confirm the architecture, won't confirm the parameter count, won't confirm anything. what we know: it passed the bar exam, it's probably MoE, and it completely redefined what 'useful AI' means. RLHF alignment plus scale produced something qualitatively different from GPT-3.

comparisons.gpt2: GPT-2 is the NPC ancestor. GPT-4 is what happens 4 years and billions of dollars later.
comparisons.gpt3: GPT-4 is GPT-3 plus RLHF plus multimodal plus secrecy. bigger, better, classified.
comparisons.llama2: GPT-4 is still the benchmark LLaMA 2 measures itself against.
comparisons.claude3: the eternal matchup. OpenAI vs Anthropic, alignment vs capabilities, both converging on the same benchmarks.
comparisons.llama3-instruct: GPT-4 is what LLaMA 3 Instruct is benchmarking against. open-source catching up but not there yet.
comparisons.deepseek-v3: GPT-4 is the closed standard. DeepSeek V3 says you can get close for a fraction of the cost.
comparisons.mixtral-8x7b: GPT-4 is the ceiling. Mixtral is the efficient challenger — not beating it but getting surprisingly close on a budget.
comparisons.grok-1: GPT-4 is OpenAI's flagship. Grok-1 is what xAI built to compete, openly, chaotically.
